title: Helping a Friend Through a Tough Breakup

all.en: When my best friend Rachel broke up with her boyfriend of five years, she was completely devastated.
all.zh: 當我最好的朋友瑞秋和交往五年的男友分手時，她整個人都崩潰了。

all.en: She called me late at night, her voice shaking and full of tears.
all.zh: 她在深夜打電話給我，聲音顫抖、滿是淚水。

all.en: Without thinking twice, I grabbed my coat and rushed over to her apartment.
all.zh: 我想都沒想，就抓起外套衝去她的公寓。

all.en: I found her sitting on the floor, surrounded by tissues and old photographs.
all.zh: 我發現她坐在地板上，身邊圍繞著面紙和舊照片。

all.en: Instead of giving advice, I simply sat beside her and let her cry on my shoulder.
all.zh: 我沒有給她建議，只是靜靜地坐在她身邊，讓她靠在我肩上哭泣。

all.en: Over the next few weeks, I made sure she ate proper meals and got enough sleep.
all.zh: 接下來的幾週，我確保她有好好吃飯、睡眠充足。

all.en: We watched silly comedies, took long walks in the park, and tried new hobbies together.
all.zh: 我們一起看搞笑的喜劇、在公園散步，並嘗試新的興趣。

all.en: Gradually, the light returned to her eyes and she began to laugh again.
all.zh: 漸漸地，她眼中的光彩回來了，她也開始能再次歡笑。

all.en: She even joined a pottery class and discovered a hidden talent she never knew she had.
all.zh: 她甚至報名了陶藝課，發現了她從不知道自己擁有的隱藏天賦。

all.en: One quiet evening, she thanked me for never giving up on her during her darkest days.
all.zh: 在一個寧靜的夜晚，她感謝我在她最黑暗的日子裡從未放棄她。

all.en: I realized that being a true friend sometimes means just being present, not having all the answers.
all.zh: 我體悟到，當一個真正的朋友，有時只是陪伴在身邊，而不是要有所有的答案。

all.en: Three months after the breakup, Rachel asked me to join her at a weekend art fair.
all.zh: 分手三個月後，瑞秋邀我和她一起去參加一場週末的藝術市集。

all.en: She had decided to sell her ceramic bowls at a small handmade goods booth.
all.zh: 她決定在一個小小的手作攤位上販售她的陶瓷碗。

all.en: I helped her set up the table early in the morning before the crowds arrived.
all.zh: 我在人潮抵達前，一大早就幫她把桌子布置好。

all.en: Her face lit up when the first customer praised the unique blue glaze on her work.
all.zh: 當第一位顧客稱讚她作品上獨特的藍色釉彩時，她的臉龐立刻發亮。

all.en: By the afternoon, she had nearly sold every piece she had carefully prepared.
all.zh: 到了下午，她精心準備的作品幾乎全部售完。

all.en: We celebrated with cold lemonade and shared a giant slice of chocolate cake.
all.zh: 我們以冰涼的檸檬水慶祝，並共享了一塊巨大的巧克力蛋糕。

all.en: She told me that pottery had become more than a hobby; it had become healing.
all.zh: 她告訴我陶藝已不只是興趣，而成為了一種療癒。

all.en: That night, she finally deleted the last folder of photos from her old relationship.
all.zh: 那晚，她終於刪掉了舊感情中最後一個資料夾的照片。

all.en: Watching her smile again reminded me how strong human hearts can truly become.
all.zh: 看著她再次微笑，提醒了我人心可以變得多麼堅強。

all.en: Through her recovery, I also learned the quiet power of patience and gentle support.
all.zh: 在她復原的過程中，我也學到了耐心與溫柔陪伴所擁有的安靜力量。

all.en: By the next spring, Rachel had quit her stressful job and opened a small pottery studio.
all.zh: 到了隔年春天，瑞秋辭去了壓力沉重的工作，開了一間小小的陶藝工作室。

all.en: She rented a sunlit corner space near a quiet canal lined with cherry blossom trees.
all.zh: 她在一條兩旁種滿櫻花樹的安靜運河旁，租了一個陽光明亮的角落空間。

all.en: I helped her paint the walls a soft cream and install wooden shelves for her bowls.
all.zh: 我幫她把牆面漆成柔和的奶油色，並裝上展示陶碗的木製層架。

all.en: On opening day, friends and neighbors lined up around the corner to support her.
all.zh: 開幕當天，朋友和鄰居從街角排隊到轉角來支持她。

all.en: She offered free clay sessions for children every Saturday afternoon in her studio.
all.zh: 她每週六下午都在工作室為孩子們提供免費的陶土體驗課。

all.en: Watching small hands shape clay seemed to heal something deep within her own heart.
all.zh: 看著孩子們的小手塑形陶土，似乎也療癒了她內心深處的某個角落。

all.en: One afternoon, a kind man named Eric came in to buy a teapot for his mother.
all.zh: 一個下午，一位名叫艾瑞克的好心男子走了進來，想為他媽媽買一個茶壺。

all.en: They struck up an easy conversation, and soon he was visiting the studio every weekend.
all.zh: 他們聊得很自然，沒過多久他就每個週末都來工作室。

all.en: Rachel told me she was not ready to date again, but she had found peace.
all.zh: 瑞秋告訴我她還沒準備好再次戀愛，但她已經找到了內心的平靜。

all.en: That, more than anything else, was the most beautiful recovery I had ever witnessed.
all.zh: 那份平靜，遠勝一切，是我見過最美麗的康復。

all.en: As summer arrived, Rachel decided her studio could finally host a small weekend exhibition.
all.zh: 夏天來臨時，瑞秋決定她的工作室終於可以舉辦一場小型的週末展覽。

all.en: She chose the theme of healing, hoping to share her own story through quiet ceramic pieces.
all.zh: 她以療癒為主題，希望透過寧靜的陶藝作品分享自己的故事。

all.en: I helped her write short cards describing the emotion behind every bowl and gentle vase.
all.zh: 我幫她寫了簡短的卡片，描述每個碗與溫柔花瓶背後的情感。

all.en: Eric quietly built sturdy wooden stands so each piece could be viewed from every angle.
all.zh: 艾瑞克靜靜地打造了堅固的木製展架，讓每件作品都能從各個角度被欣賞。

all.en: On opening night, dozens of strangers came and stayed long after the official closing time.
all.zh: 開幕之夜，數十位陌生人前來，並在官方閉展時間後仍久久停留。

all.en: A young woman cried softly in front of a small blue bowl shaped like cupped hands.
all.zh: 一位年輕女子在一個形似捧起雙手的小藍碗前輕聲落淚。

all.en: Rachel hugged her and quietly gifted the piece, refusing to accept any payment in return.
all.zh: 瑞秋擁抱了她，靜靜地把作品送給她，並拒絕收取任何費用。

all.en: By midnight, every visitor had written a sentence on the studio's growing hopeful message wall.
all.zh: 到了午夜，每位訪客都在工作室那面不斷成長的希望留言牆上寫下一句話。

all.en: Eric and Rachel went on their first quiet dinner the following Thursday evening together.
all.zh: 艾瑞克與瑞秋在隔週的星期四晚上，一同享用了第一次安靜的晚餐。

all.en: Healing, I finally understood, is not a single moment but a long, gentle journey of choices.
all.zh: 我終於明白，療癒不是單一的時刻，而是一段漫長、溫柔且充滿選擇的旅程。

all.en: By the next autumn, Rachel and Eric had quietly become close companions through countless small moments.
all.zh: 到了隔年秋天，瑞秋和艾瑞克透過無數個小小的時刻，悄悄成為親密的伴侶。

all.en: He attended every studio class and learned to wedge clay with the patient care of a beginner.
all.zh: 他參加了她工作室的每一堂課，以初學者的耐心學會了揉土這個步驟。

all.en: She taught children's classes with him on Saturdays, and they shared lunches at the nearby canal park.
all.zh: 她星期六和他一起教兒童課，他們在附近運河公園共享午餐。

all.en: On a quiet October evening, Eric proposed using a small ceramic ring Rachel had once thrown for fun.
all.zh: 在一個寧靜的十月夜晚，艾瑞克用瑞秋曾隨手做的一只小陶瓷戒指，向她求婚。

all.en: She laughed and cried at once, finally saying yes with both hands still dusted with white clay.
all.zh: 她又笑又哭，最終帶著雙手仍沾著白色陶土的模樣答應了他。

all.en: They planned a tiny garden wedding with only forty guests, all close family and dear chosen friends.
all.zh: 他們規劃了一場小巧的花園婚禮，只邀請了四十位賓客，全是親近的家人與摯愛的朋友。

all.en: Rachel asked me to be her maid of honor and walked down the aisle to a soft piano melody.
all.zh: 瑞秋邀我擔任她的伴娘，並在輕柔的鋼琴旋律中走過紅毯。

all.en: During my toast, I told their guests how broken hearts can quietly become bridges to bigger love.
all.zh: 敬酒時，我告訴賓客們，破碎的心如何能靜靜地化為通往更大的愛的橋樑。

all.en: Eric hugged me tightly afterward and thanked me for staying beside Rachel during her hardest year.
all.zh: 艾瑞克之後緊緊擁抱我，感謝我在瑞秋最艱難的那一年裡始終陪伴在她身邊。

all.en: Watching them dance under quiet garden lights, I knew healing had finally turned into a new beginning.
all.zh: 看著他們在寧靜的花園燈光下共舞，我知道療癒終於化為了一個全新的開始。
